name: Cy Touff
abstract: Cyril James Touff (March 4, 1927 in Chicago – January 24, 2003 in Evanston, Illinois) was an American jazz bass trumpeter. He was one of the few jazz musicians known as a bass trumpeter.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Goin' to Chicago

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: Cy Touf was playing second trombone... I played first trombone... And that first night Woody calls up “Four Others.”... So I did this thing... So then I was on Woody’s band
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: No, that was under Cy Touff's name. And that was for Pacific Jazz, you know. And I did so much recording out here...

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: And we had good trombones, Cy Touff, Dennis Farnan... So we always kept an -- but it didn't make any difference

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: And he had people like Cy Touff was with him, the bass trumpeter...
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
